# Plan: Course-Keyed Tutor Shell With Studio, Sessions, Schedule, Publish, Popouts, and Dictation

## Summary
Ship a production-ready course-backed workflow where `Brain` remains home, `/tutor` becomes the single project/session shell, `Project == existing course_id`, `Session == existing tutor_session`, and the Tutor shell exposes four modes: `Studio`, `Tutor`, `Schedule`, and `Publish`.

Locked v1 decisions:
- No `course_projects` table in v1. Project identity is the existing `courses.id`.
- No standalone `/studio` route in v1. Studio lives inside `/tutor`.
- `/tutor` remains the public route; deep linking uses query params: `course_id`, `session_id`, `mode`, `board_scope`, optional `board_id`.
- `Overall Summary Board` stays in v1, but only as an aggregated rollup of promoted course/session items. No global-only Studio items.
- Dictation is Chromium-first best-effort only, with feature-detected fallback elsewhere.
- Popout scope is limited to `viewer` and `current note`.
- Existing Tutor, Calendar, syllabus, vault, and video APIs remain backward-compatible for one rollout wave.

Important interface and persistence changes:
- Add `project_workspace_state(course_id UNIQUE, active_tutor_session_id, last_mode, active_board_scope, active_board_id, viewer_state_json, selected_material_ids_json, revision, updated_at)`.
- Add normalized Studio tables keyed by `course_id` and `tutor_session_id`:
  - `studio_items`
  - `studio_item_revisions`
  - `studio_boards`
  - `studio_board_entries`
  - `studio_actions`
- Add a thin shell-summary API for Tutor shell launch/restore and mode-specific APIs for Studio, Schedule, and Publish.
- Add Studio commands: `capture`, `compact`, `promote`, `restore`, `board membership/layout`.
- Add Schedule aggregate APIs over existing `course_events`.
- Add Publish APIs for promoted project resources only.
- Add feature flags and telemetry for project shell, Studio writes, schedule ship, publish, popouts, and dictation.

## Task Breakdown
### Phase 1: Contract and feasibility gates
- `T1 Canon and navigation freeze` (`orchestrator/docs`, deps: none, parallel: no): lock `Brain -> /tutor -> course_id project -> tutor_session -> mode`, clarify Studio/Tutor/Schedule/Publish ownership, shell state vs teaching state, and Overall Summary Board as aggregated rollup only. Verify by doc checklist across canon, architecture, and workboard.
- `T2 Schema/API freeze` (`backend-contract`, deps: `T1`, parallel: no): freeze the exact course-keyed schema, query-param route contract, shell-summary API, mode-specific endpoints, feature flags, telemetry fields, and backward-compat write bridge to `artifacts_json`, `quick_notes`, and `card_drafts`. Verify by contract checklist and request/response fixtures.
- `T3 Migration and compatibility proof` (`backend-tests`, deps: `T2`, parallel: no): add migration tests for new Studio/shell tables, mixed-version reads, null/default backfills, idempotent reruns, and old frontend payload compatibility. Verify with targeted backend tests and frontend API tests.

### Phase 2: Backend foundations
- `T4 Course-keyed project read model` (`backend-schema`, deps: `T3`, parallel: no): add course-keyed shell summary and continuation APIs without introducing a project wrapper table. Include active session, recent captures, pending schedule counts, publish counts, and last mode. Verify with route/API tests.
- `T5 Shell state separation from Tutor teaching state` (`backend-session`, deps: `T3`, `T4`, parallel: no): persist `project_workspace_state`, launch/resume/open-mode semantics, and restore precedence rules so mode switches never mutate live Tutor session state. Verify with Tutor session-linking and E2E session tests.
- `T6 Studio persistence and commands` (`backend-studio`, deps: `T3`, `T4`, `T5`, parallel: yes`): add normalized Studio item/board tables, revision history, action audit, `capture/compact/promote/restore` APIs, and idempotency keys. Verify with artifact routing, Obsidian IO, and artifact certification tests.
- `T7 Schedule, publish backend, flags, telemetry` (`backend-integrations`, deps: `T4`, `T5`, `T6`, parallel: yes`): add course-keyed schedule aggregate over `course_events`, promoted-resource publish APIs, feature flags, request IDs, and partial-failure reporting. Verify with calendar, vault, and artifact tests.

### Phase 3: Brain launch and Tutor shell
- `T8 Brain Projects Dashboard` (`frontend-brain`, deps: `T4`, `T7`, parallel: yes`): add recent courses-as-projects, active sessions, quick continue, and cross-project schedule launch from Brain. Verify with `brain.test.tsx`.
- `T9 Tutor course/session shell` (`frontend-shell`, deps: `T5`, `T8`, parallel: no`): turn `/tutor` into the shell with query-param deep links, tabs for `Studio/Tutor/Schedule/Publish`, distinct mode headers, session start chooser, restore behavior, and stale-state fallback. Verify with Tutor page tests and workspace integration tests.

### Phase 4: Studio and workbench
- `T10 Inbox-first Studio and three board scopes` (`frontend-studio`, deps: `T6`, `T9`, parallel: yes`): implement `Inbox`, `Session Summary Board`, `Project Summary Board`, and editable `Overall Summary Board` as aggregated rollup only. Define exact item-state rules for `captured`, `boarded`, `promoted`, `archived`. Verify with Studio/Tutor artifact tests.
- `T11 Capture flows from Tutor and workbench` (`frontend-capture`, deps: `T6`, `T9`, `T10`, parallel: no`): add `To Studio -> Note` and `To Studio -> Summary Board`, capture from notes/graph/table/canvas/cards, and `Copy/Move` promotion dialog. Verify with Tutor chat and workspace integration tests.
- `T12 Viewers, clipping, and dictation` (`frontend-viewers`, deps: `T9`, `T10`, `T11`, parallel: yes`): add PDF.js viewer, readability-first DOCX rendering with `docx-preview` and plain-text fallback, MP4 viewer with timestamp capture, source clipping with provenance, and Chromium-first dictation into current note with unsupported-state handling. Verify with Tutor workspace tests plus video process/enrich tests.

### Phase 5: Schedule and publish UX
- `T13 Project Schedule and Calendar aggregate UX` (`frontend-schedule`, deps: `T7`, `T9`, parallel: yes`): build project-local Schedule mode for syllabus import, date review, and ship-to-calendar while preserving Calendar as the cross-project rollup page. Verify with syllabus/calendar tests.
- `T14 Publish mode and promoted-resource workflow` (`frontend-publish`, deps: `T7`, `T10`, `T11`, parallel: yes`): add readiness states, project resources list, publish actions to Obsidian/Anki/Brain, and partial-failure retry UI. Verify with vault/artifact tests.

### Phase 6: Popouts, hardening, and release proof
- `T15 Popout transport and edit safety` (`frontend-state`, deps: `T12`, `T14`, parallel: no`): add same-origin `BroadcastChannel` sync, viewer popout as read-only, note popout edit handshake, conflict banner behavior, and close/reopen recovery. Verify with Tutor page/state tests.
- `T16 Failure-path coverage and live smoke` (`orchestrator/hardening`, deps: `T13`, `T14`, `T15`, parallel: no`): add negative-path tests for SSE interruption, stale restore, dictation unsupported/failure, popout failure, partial publish failure, calendar/provider failures, and final full-circle smoke path. Verify with targeted frontend/backend suites plus `Start_Dashboard.bat` live smoke.

## Agent Swarm Execution
Use `swarm-waves` with one orchestrator and specialized workers. No two parallel workers should edit the same primary file set.

- `Wave A`:
  - `Orchestrator` runs `T1`
- `Wave B`:
  - `Backend-contract worker` runs `T2`
- `Wave C`:
  - `Backend-test worker` runs `T3`
- `Wave D`:
  - `Backend-schema worker` runs `T4`
- `Wave E`:
  - `Backend-session worker` runs `T5`
- `Wave F` in parallel:
  - `Backend-studio worker` runs `T6`
  - `Frontend-brain worker` runs `T8`
- `Wave G`:
  - `Backend-integrations worker` runs `T7`
- `Wave H`:
  - `Frontend-shell worker` runs `T9`
- `Wave I` in parallel:
  - `Frontend-studio worker` runs `T10`
  - `Frontend-schedule worker` runs `T13`
- `Wave J`:
  - `Frontend-capture worker` runs `T11`
- `Wave K` in parallel:
  - `Frontend-viewers worker` runs `T12`
  - `Frontend-publish worker` runs `T14`
- `Wave L`:
  - `Frontend-state worker` runs `T15`
- `Wave M`:
  - `Hardening/release worker` runs `T16`

Worker handoff contract:
- Each worker must receive: final goal, locked v1 decisions, exact task ID, allowed write scope, dependencies already satisfied, verification command(s), and rejection criteria.
- Each worker must return: summary of changes, files changed, tests run/results, manual checks, flags touched, blockers.
- Reject worker output if it introduces a freeform project model, adds a new public route for Studio, stores major Studio state in a single blob, or marks work done without the listed verification evidence.

## Test Plan
- Migration and compatibility:
  - mixed-version reads with existing Tutor rows
  - coexistence with `artifacts_json`, `quick_notes`, `card_drafts`
  - idempotent migrations and rollback-safe backfills
- Routing and restore:
  - `/tutor?course_id=...&session_id=...&mode=...`
  - invalid query params fall back safely
  - refresh preserves shell state without corrupting Tutor turns
- Studio behavior:
  - Inbox-first rules
  - Session/Project/Overall board behavior
  - Overall board edits promoted items only, no global-only raw items
  - `Copy` and `Move` promotion paths
- Schedule and publish:
  - syllabus import and review
  - ship-to-calendar success/failure
  - publish readiness, partial failure, retry
- Viewers, dictation, and popouts:
  - PDF success
  - DOCX render success and plain-text fallback
  - MP4 timestamped capture
  - Chromium dictation supported path
  - unsupported browser and permission denied paths
  - BroadcastChannel success and note-popout handshake failure paths
- Full-circle live proof:
  - `Brain -> course launch -> session create -> Tutor turn -> To Studio capture -> Studio cleanup -> Schedule ship -> Publish -> restore -> end -> delete`

## Assumptions and defaults
- `Project == course_id` in v1.
- `Session == existing tutor_session`.
- `/tutor` remains the only public Tutor route.
- `Overall Summary Board` is editable in v1, but only as an aggregated rollup of promoted course/session items.
- Dictation is assistive, not release-blocking across all browsers; Chromium desktop is the only promised path.
- PDF and DOCX support in v1 are reading/capture tools, not fidelity-perfect document editors.

# Plan: Course-Keyed Tutor Shell With Studio, Sessions, Schedule, Publish, Popouts, and Dictation

## Summary
Ship a production-ready course-backed workflow where `Brain` stays home, `/tutor` becomes the single project/session shell, `Project == existing courses.id`, and `Session == existing tutor_sessions.session_id`.

Locked v1 decisions:
- No `course_projects` table in v1.
- No standalone `/studio` route in v1. Studio lives inside `/tutor`.
- `/tutor` remains the public route; deep linking uses query params: `course_id`, `session_id`, `mode`, `board_scope`, optional `board_id`.
- Tutor shell modes are `Studio`, `Tutor`, `Schedule`, and `Publish`.
- `Overall Summary Board` stays in v1, but only as an aggregated rollup of promoted course/session items. No global-only raw Studio items.
- Dictation is Chromium-first best-effort only, with explicit unsupported-state UX.
- Popouts are limited to `viewer` and `current note`.
- Existing Tutor, Calendar, syllabus, vault, video, `artifacts_json`, `quick_notes`, and `card_drafts` contracts remain backward-compatible for one rollout wave.

Conductor vs planner split:
- This work is multi-phase, cross-subsystem, and architecture-heavy, so it must open or update a Conductor track first.
- The full durable plan lives in the Conductor track (`spec.md`, `plan.md`, optional `review.md`).
- Planner-backed queue tasks are optional and only generated for the first explicit unblocked wave after the revised plan is accepted.

## Key Changes
Public interfaces and persistence:
- Add `project_workspace_state(course_id UNIQUE, active_tutor_session_id, last_mode, active_board_scope, active_board_id, viewer_state_json, selected_material_ids_json, revision, updated_at)`.
- Add normalized Studio tables keyed by `course_id` and `tutor_session_id`:
  - `studio_items`
  - `studio_item_revisions`
  - `studio_boards`
  - `studio_board_entries`
  - `studio_actions`
- Keep `/tutor` as the only public route and add URL query contract:
  - `course_id`
  - `session_id`
  - `mode=studio|tutor|schedule|publish`
  - `board_scope=session|project|overall`
  - optional `board_id`
- Replace the broad “project summary endpoint” with:
  - one thin shell-summary endpoint for project identity, active session, continuation state, and counts
  - mode-specific detail endpoints for Studio, Schedule, and Publish
- Add Studio commands:
  - `capture`
  - `compact`
  - `promote`
  - `restore`
  - `board membership/layout`
- Add Schedule aggregate APIs over existing `course_events`.
- Add Publish APIs for promoted project resources only.
- Viewer stack:
  - PDF via `pdfjs-dist`
  - DOCX via `docx-preview` with plain-text fallback
  - MP4 via native HTML5 video plus existing video pipeline
- Popout rules:
  - viewer popout is read-only
  - current-note popout becomes editable only after successful same-origin `BroadcastChannel` handshake
- Dictation:
  - browser-native, Chromium-first best-effort only
  - no raw audio storage
  - clear unsupported and permission-denied states

# Plan: Course-Keyed Tutor Shell With Studio, Sessions, Schedule, Publish, Popouts, and Dictation

## Summary
Ship a production-ready course-backed workflow where `Brain` stays home, `/tutor` becomes the single project/session shell, `Project == existing courses.id`, and `Session == existing tutor_sessions.session_id`.

Locked v1 decisions:
- No `course_projects` table in v1.
- No standalone `/studio` route in v1. Studio lives inside `/tutor`.
- `/tutor` remains the public route; deep linking uses query params: `course_id`, `session_id`, `mode`, `board_scope`, optional `board_id`.
- Tutor shell modes are `Studio`, `Tutor`, `Schedule`, and `Publish`.
- `Overall Summary Board` stays in v1, but only as an aggregated rollup of promoted course/session items. No global-only raw Studio items.
- Dictation is Chromium-first best-effort only, with explicit unsupported-state UX.
- Popouts are limited to `viewer` and `current note`.
- Existing Tutor, Calendar, syllabus, vault, video, `artifacts_json`, `quick_notes`, and `card_drafts` contracts remain backward-compatible for one rollout wave.

Conductor vs planner split:
- This work is multi-phase, cross-subsystem, and architecture-heavy, so it must open or update a Conductor track first.
- The full durable plan lives in the Conductor track (`spec.md`, `plan.md`, optional `review.md`).
- Planner-backed queue tasks are optional and only generated for the first explicit unblocked wave after the revised plan is accepted.

- `course_id` is the live Tutor scope key today, but product-shell analytics/privacy are keyed by `workspace_id` instead, not by course, in [db_setup.py](/C:/pt-study-sop/brain/db_setup.py#L135) and [api_product.py](/C:/pt-study-sop/brain/dashboard/api_product.py#L17). A course-keyed shell should not overload `workspace_id`; keep `course_key` as the shell selector and pass `workspaceId` only for product analytics if needed.

Tutor create uses `mode` for user-facing study mode and `phase` for lifecycle, but tests still pass odd values and turn payloads also use a `mode` object for capability flags in [api.types.ts](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/api.types.ts#L1051), [api_tutor_sessions.py](/C:/pt-study-sop/brain/dashboard/api_tutor_sessions.py#L145), and [test_api_tutor_mode_flags.py](/C:/pt-study-sop/brain/tests/test_api_tutor_mode_flags.py#L92). The route shell is simple and stable. [`App.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/App.tsx#L18) defines the public pages and keeps them alive once visited; `/brain` is normalized to `/` in the router alias logic at [`App.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/App.tsx#L29). The shared shell is [`layout.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/layout.tsx#L47): it owns the top nav, the global quick-notes drawer, and the full-height page frame for Brain/Tutor at [`layout.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/layout.tsx#L657).

Brain is thin at the page level. [`brain.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/pages/brain.tsx#L7) just wraps [`useBrainWorkspace.ts`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/brain/useBrainWorkspace.ts#L38) and [`MainContent.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/brain/MainContent.tsx#L18) inside `Layout`. Brain’s public modes are already constrained to `home | profile` in [`useBrainWorkspace.ts`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/brain/useBrainWorkspace.ts#L6) and rendered as the two tab panels in [`MainContent.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/brain/MainContent.tsx#L10).

Tutor is the opposite: the page owns almost all orchestration. [`tutor.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/pages/tutor.tsx#L210) carries restore/handoff/session state, localStorage/sessionStorage contracts, session lifecycle, header/toolbars, and the main content switch. The current Tutor shell is effectively:
- `Wizard` state via `showSetup` at [`tutor.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/pages/tutor.tsx#L272)
- `Chat` state via `!showSetup && !showWorkspace`
- `Workspace` state via `showWorkspace` at [`tutor.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/pages/tutor.tsx#L271)
- `Artifacts` as a right-side overlay via `showArtifacts` at [`tutor.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/pages/tutor.tsx#L270)

The concrete Tutor content switch happens in the render block at [`tutor.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/pages/tutor.tsx#L1511):
- `TutorWizard` for setup/resume
- `TutorWorkspaceSurface` for workspace tools
- `TutorChat` for the live session
- `TutorArtifacts` as a side panel, not a primary mode

The child surfaces are already fairly separate:
- [`TutorWorkspaceSurface.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/TutorWorkspaceSurface.tsx#L31) is a self-contained “studio/tools” host with internal tabs `notes|canvas|graph|table` at [`TutorWorkspaceSurface.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/TutorWorkspaceSurface.tsx#L16), reusing Brain internals like `VaultEditor`, `ExcalidrawCanvas`, and `GraphPanel`.
- [`TutorChat.tsx`](/C:/pt-study-sop/dashboard_rebuild/client/src/components/TutorChat.tsx#L32) is the live session UI and streaming client; it mainly depends on `sessionId`, content filter props, artifact callback, and `useSSEStream`.
